subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear section headers and a contents page guide the reader through the investigation
Logical narrative with introduction, body and conclusion present
weaknesses: Duplicate numbering in the table of contents and abrupt transitions interrupt flow
Some repetition and superfluous explanations limit conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and well structured overall, but recurring minor flaws (duplicate section numbers, overly long paragraphs, occasional redundancy) prevent it from reaching the higher bands. A Band 2 score (relevant and somewhat concise with basic structure) best reflects its strengths and limitations.

strengths: Effective use of tables, boxplots and other representations with labelled axes
Appropriate notation used at times (e.g., for means and standard deviations)
weaknesses: Inconsistent and nonstandard symbols (Î©, Î¸, âIORâ) create confusion
Contradictory statements and formatting glitches reduce clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student uses multiple representations effectively, inconsistent notation and contradictory descriptions undermine clarity and precision. This places the work in Band 2 (some appropriate language and representations, but with inconsistencies).

strengths: The exploration is driven by genuine personal context (teenage girlâs experience)
Designing and administering the survey shows independent thinking
weaknesses: Procedures and analyses follow standard methods without extensive innovation
Creativity is present but remains within expected statistical techniques
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates a clear personal approach and significant initiative in collecting and analyzing original data. However, the methods remain fairly conventional, so Band 2 (clear personal approach and significant creativity) is appropriate.

strengths: Meaningful reflections on skewness, outliers and sample limitations appear throughout
Connections are made between statistical findings and broader implications
weaknesses: Analysis remains descriptive and lacks deeper critique of methodology
Limited discussion of potential biases (e.g., self-selection, response errors)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is sustained reflection on the investigationâs limitations and implications, demonstrating Band 2 performance (meaningful reflection with some critique), but it falls short of the deep critical analysis required for the next level.

strengths: Relevant SL-level methods are applied (means, medians, SD, IQR, t-test)
Interpretations of p-value and distributional shape show conceptual understanding
weaknesses: Major numerical errors (incorrect IQR, mis-typed formulas) undermine accuracy
Key assumptions (equal variances) are untested and some formulae misused
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration uses course-level mathematics competently but is hampered by partial correctness due to calculation errors and untested assumptions. These flaws place it in Band 2 (some relevant mathematics at course level, but only partially correct).

strandSelections.reasoning: Designing and administering a primary survey, choosing statistical tools and framing hypotheses show noticeable independent thinking beyond following a template, but not to an outstanding degree.  Band 2 is appropriate.

Explanations of skewness, interpretation of p-value and discussion of outliers show a reasonable grasp of statistical ideas, though occasional misconceptions persist.  Understanding is good in parts rather than throughout, corresponding to band 3.

Several numerical errors are evident: IQR for girls incorrectly given as 1 when quartiles are 6 and 7; symbols mis-typed; bell-curve parameters not justified.  Roughly half the calculations appear correct, so accuracy is âpartially correctâ â band 2.

Most text is directly related to the research question.  Occasional repetition (e.g. multiple restatements that girls use social media more) and some superfluous explanations of basic terms limit conciseness, yet irrelevant material is rare; hence band 3 â relevant and mostly concise.

The student employs notation for mean, SD, hypotheses etc., but there are errors and inconsistencies: misuse of symbols (Î©, Î¸) in frequency table, âIORâ, â03-01â, and fragmented formulas.  Appropriate language appears only âsomeâ of the time, placing the work in band 2.

Narrative is generally understandable, yet inconsistent symbol use and occasional formatting glitches reduce clarity.  Descriptions sometimes contradict earlier numbers (e.g. maximum equal yet later âhigher maximum for femalesâ).  Therefore communication is âmostly clear with some consistencyâ (band 2).

Means, medians, SD, IQRs, boxplots and a pooled two-sample t-test are all at SL course level and relevant to the question.  Use is competent but not especially sophisticated; therefore band 3 (relevant mathematics at course level used).

Tables, bar chart, boxplots and bell-curve graphs are all used and support the discussion.  Axes are labelled and gender is colour-coded, so representations are effective even if not sophisticated.  This aligns with band 3 for multiple representations.
The student reflects on skewness, outliers and sample limitations, indicating meaningfulânot merely tokenâreflection across the work.  Depth remains modest, fitting band 2.

Creating original data, multiple visualisations and carrying out a t-test demonstrate some initiative and creativity, though techniques remain standard.  Hence âsignificant creativity and initiativeâ â band 2.

The report follows a clear narrative: background and hypothesis, data collection, descriptive statistics, inferential test, bell-curve check, limitations and conclusion.  Each step builds on the previous one and the reader can follow the intention without major gaps.  However, some transitions are abrupt (e.g. suddenly repeating â5.â in the table of contents) and a few statements are misplaced, so the logical flow is good rather than exemplary.

There is some analysis of why sample size, grouped data and single-school source may bias results.  However, discussion is descriptive rather than deeply critical, so âmeaningful analysis with some critiqueâ (band 2) is the best match.

Reflections clearly link back to understanding of gender differences and mental-health implications; nevertheless, they do not substantially deepen mathematical insight.  Band 2 â clear connection â is appropriate.

A contents page, numbered sections, headings and captions demonstrate deliberate structure.  Introduction, body and conclusion are all present and identifiable.  Minor flaws (duplicate section numbers, appendix split over two pages) prevent a seamless feel, so âwell organisedâ (band 3) is the best fit.

The exploration is clearly driven by the studentâs personal experience as âa teenage girl in 2024â; this perspective shapes question choice and discussion, satisfying the descriptor for a âclear personal approachâ (band 2).

annotations.comment: The clear and descriptive section header establishes an immediate context for the investigation, aiding reader orientation.

The introductory paragraph is overly long and contains some repetitive phrases. Consider breaking it into smaller paragraphs focused on purpose, personal context, and methodology for better conciseness.
The table of contents contains duplicate numbering for section 5. Correcting section numbering will improve navigation and coherence.
The studentâs vivid personal account of social media experiences demonstrates genuine engagement and drives the originality of the exploration.
The bar chart lacks a legend explaining colour coding for genders. Adding a clear legend will make the representation self-contained and immediately interpretable.
Notation in the summary table is inconsistent (e.g., overlines, bolding). Standardize representation of quartiles and modes to avoid ambiguity.
Use of nonstandard symbols like Î© and Î¸ in the frequency table creates confusion. Replace with numeric values and consistent notation (e.g., integers) for clarity.
The standalone symbol â4â appears out of context. Ensure all inline math is properly introduced or removed if not relevant to the analysis.
The formula for girlsâ mean references summation to infinity incorrectly. Use the finite sum formula $$\bar x=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n x_i$$ and substitute the actual terms.
Description of box plots misstates quartile ranges (e.g., âwomen spend 6-7 hoursâ). Clarify exact Q1, median and Q3 values instead of broad ranges.
Interpretation claims both maximum values are equal, then contradicts by saying femalesâ maximum is higher. Review earlier statistics and ensure consistency in narrative.
Use of âIORâ instead of âIQRâ and â03â01â is nonstandard. Correct to $$IQR = Q3 - Q1$$ and label variables clearly.
Consider the possibility of response bias or data-entry errors even with primary data; reflecting on these would strengthen critical evaluation.
The assumption of equal variances in the t-test is untested. Include an F-test or Leveneâs test to justify pooling variances.
The tabular summary of sample means and standard deviations is clearly formatted, aiding quick comprehension of key values.
Adding a confidence interval around the mean difference would deepen the inferential analysis beyond p-values alone.
The bell-curve plot lacks clear annotation of the mean and standard deviation parameters used. Label these to connect the visual to the underlying statistics.
The interpretation of the boysâ bell curve contradicts earlier skewness findings. Reconcile this inconsistency by reviewing raw distributions.
Consider discussing self-selection bias and the limitations of an online survey to demonstrate deeper critical reflection.
The discussion of distributional skewness repeats earlier observations. Focus on new insights or statistical evidence to avoid redundancy.
The formula for boysâ mean omits explicit summation notation and denominator. Introduce $$\bar{x}=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n x_i$$ to clarify computation.
The conclusion restates findings but could be strengthened by explicitly linking insights back to implications for mental-health interventions.
The placement of the session date is ambiguous. Consider integrating it into a header or removing if extraneous for cleaner presentation.
Explicitly numbering the âIntroductionâ header ensures readers can follow the structure; this clear labeling supports logical development.
The descriptive-analysis section header is informative and guides the reader through your methodological steps effectively.
